channel.title: 8bitconcepts Research
channel.description: Practitioner-grade research on AI engineering, hiring, compensation, and infrastructure. Auto-refreshed weekly.

item.title: Q2 2026 AI Engineering Compensation by Skill
item.description: Research roles pay a $42k premium over generative-AI roles ($274k vs $231k avg), even though generative-AI has 2.5x more openings.

item.title: Q2 2026 AI Engineering Hiring Snapshot
item.description: Live snapshot: 8,618 AI/ML engineering roles across 513 companies, $213k median, 599 new this week. OpenAI leads with 336 open roles.

item.title: Q2 2026 The Junior AI Hiring Gap
item.description: Only ~7% of AI/ML engineering roles are open to juniors. For every entry-level opening there are ~10 senior-plus roles - the tightest junior-to-senior ratio in tech.

item.title: Q2 2026 MCP Ecosystem Health
item.description: 5,578 agent-ready sites indexed, only 575 (10.3%) pass a live JSON-RPC handshake. Category breakdown and the regulated verticals still waiting to be built.

item.title: Q2 2026 Remote vs Onsite AI Hiring
item.description: Hybrid AI/ML roles pay a ~$35k premium over remote+onsite ($253k vs $218k). 55% of AI engineering roles still require full onsite attendance.

item.title: Beyond the Prompt
item.description: The teams shipping reliable production agentic systems are not prompting harder - they moved through a specific engineering maturity ladder.

item.title: Shift Handoff Intelligence
item.description: 100% information retention with AI-generated shift briefings vs. 40-60% with verbal handoffs. The pattern-detection gap is where preventable failures originate.

item.title: The Agentic Accountability Gap
item.description: Enterprise teams spent three years learning how to stop AI from saying the wrong thing. Then they handed those same systems write-access to production.

item.title: The Guardrails Gap
item.description: Engineering teams spent 2023 and 2024 obsessing over what AI would say. In 2026, the threat has shifted - agentic systems are now taking action.

item.title: The Hallucination Budget
item.description: Most engineering teams ship LLM features with less testing rigor than they apply to a login form. Production hallucinations land on customer trust and legal risk.

item.title: The Integration Tax
item.description: Model API costs are 10-20% of what AI actually costs to ship. Where the other 80% goes.

item.title: The Mandate Trap
item.description: Shopify's AI mandate worked. Duolingo's didn't. Companies copying the Shopify memo template are learning the wrong lesson.

item.title: The Measurement Problem
item.description: A company ran an AI system for eight months before discovering four months of silent degradation. Most have no better detection mechanism.

item.title: The Org Chart Problem
item.description: AI transformation fails because of where it sits in the org chart. Every placement encodes a ceiling.

item.title: The Six Percent
item.description: 88% of organizations use AI. Only 6% see meaningful returns. What McKinsey found in 2,000 companies across 105 countries.
